heading: Example
body:
The following index definition indexes the `title` field in the
[sample collection](custom-analyzers-eg-coll) collection using a custom
analyzer named `titleAutocomplete`. The custom analyzer specifies
the following:

- Apply the [standard](standard-tokenizer-ref) to
  create tokens based on word break rules.

- Apply the following filters on the tokens:

  - `icuFolding` token filter to apply character foldings to the
    tokens.

  - `edgeGram` token filter to create 4 to 7 character long tokens
    from the left side.

Atlas UI

Visual Editor

- In the Custom Analyzers section, click Add
  Custom Analyzer.

- Select the Create Your Own radio button and click
  Next.

- Type whitespace in the Analyzer Name field.

- Expand Tokenizer if it's collapsed.

- Select whitespace from the dropdown.

- Expand Token Filters and click 
  Add token filter.

- Select lowercase from the dropdown and click
  Add token filter to add the token filter to your
  custom analyzer.

- Click  Add token filter to add
  another token filter.

- Select spanishPluralStemming from the dropdown and type the value
  shown in the following table for the fields:

  | Field   | Value |
  | ------- | ----- |
  | minGram | 4     |
  | maxGram | 7     |

- Click Add token filter to add the token filter to your
  custom analyzer.

- Click Add to create the custom analyzer.

- In the Field Mappings section, click Add
  Field Mapping to apply the custom analyzer on
  the message field.

- Select message from the Field
  Name dropdown and dateFacet from the Data
  Type dropdown.

- In the properties section for the data type, select whitespace
  from the Index Analyzer and Search Analyzer
  dropdowns.

- Click Add, then Save Changes.

JSON Editor

Replace the default index definition with the following example:

```json
{
  "analyzer": "titleAutocomplete",
  "mappings": {
    "dynamic": false,
    "fields": {
      "title": {
        "type": "string",
        "analyzer": "titleAutocomplete"
      }
    }
  },
  "analyzers": [
    {
      "name": "titleAutocomplete",
      "charFilters": [],
      "tokenizer": {
        "type": "standard"
      },
      "tokenFilters": [
        {
          "type": "icuFolding"
        },
        {
          "type": "edgeGram",
          "minGram": 4,
          "maxGram": 7
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

MongoDB Shell

The following query searches the `title` field of the [sample collection](custom-analyzers-eg-coll) collection for terms that begin with
`mee`, followed by any number of other characters.

Atlas UI

MongoDB Shell

MongoDB Search returns documents with `_id: 1` and `_id: 3` because the
documents contain the term `meeting`, which matches the query
criteria. Specifically, MongoDB Search creates the following 4 to 7 character
tokens (searchable terms) for the documents in the results, which it
then matches to the query term `mee*`:

| Document ID | Output Tokens                                                           |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| "\_id": 1   | team, team', team's, week, weekl, weekly, meet, meeti, meetin, meeting  |
| "\_id": 3   | regu, regul, regula, regular, boar, board, meet, meeti, meetin, meeting |
